data.title: Just Women's Sports revives 'Between The Lines' with Lisa Leslie, sponsored by Intuit TurboTax. First guest: Kelsey Mitchell. LOS ANGELES, Feb. 19, 2026.
data.description: A renowned interview series, recognized for its honest discussions with top basketball players, is making a comeback with Kelsey Mitchell as the inaugural guest. The show, named Between the Lines, is produced by Just Women's Sports and is set to premiere on Feb. 19, 2026, in Los Angeles. The series has received accolades for its engaging conversations with prominent athletes, offering viewers an inside look at the lives and perspectives of basketball's biggest stars. Stay tuned for more insightful interviews and exciting moments as the series unfolds.

data.title: Negotiations resume among White House, banks, and crypto groups regarding stablecoin rewards in the digital asset market legislation.
data.description: Lawmakers are currently working on advancing legislation concerning digital-asset market structures, with stablecoin incentives being a prominent unresolved issue. The importance of stablecoin incentives is highlighted as legislators aim to address key aspects of the digital asset market. The specific details regarding the legislation and the exact incentives being discussed remain undisclosed, but it is a significant aspect with numbers indicating its relevance in shaping the future regulatory framework.

data.title: Abundia priced $20M registered direct offering of 5,934,718 common stock shares, to result in gross proceeds.
data.description: On Feb. 19, 2026, Abundia Global Impact Group, Inc. (NYSE: AGIG) revealed a new funding development. They have struck a deal with a fundamental institutional investor to sell 5,934,718 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants) in a registered direct offering. This initiative is anticipated to generate around $20 million in gross proceeds, with the closing scheduled for Feb. 23, 2026. The company, specializing in low-carbon energy solutions by converting biomass and plastics waste into valuable low-carbon fuels, aims to utilize this investment for further growth.

data.title: Nano Nuclear Energy, Inc. stock rises as Q1 loss narrows, reporting a loss of 13 cents per share (vs. expected 29 cents).
data.description: Nano Nuclear Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: NNE) saw a surge in its shares during after-hours trading on Tuesday following the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report. The report revealed a smaller loss per share than expected, with quarterly losses at 13 cents per share compared to the anticipated 29 cents per share. The company projects around $65 million in estimated cash expenditures in the upcoming period. For more information, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com. Keep an eye on NNE stock for further price movements.

data.title: Stock market sees Dow and Nasdaq gain slightly, Nasdaq breaks losing streak, gold and silver stocks fall.
data.description: Today in the stock market, the Nasdaq managed to break a losing streak but only marginally. Small-cap stocks closed unchanged after experiencing volatile trading throughout the day. The Dow Jones and Nasdaq saw slight gains. On the other hand, companies involved in gold and silver saw a decline. This information was brought to you by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Robotics sector booming with $71.2B market value; 541,302 industrial units installed in 2023. VanEck predicts growth in healthcare, logistics.
data.description: The worldwide robotics industry, estimated to be worth $71.2 billion, saw the installation of 541,302 industrial units in the year 2023. VanEck anticipates a growth trend extending beyond factory settings, with a move towards integration into logistics and healthcare sectors.
